title: Deep nested useEffect cleanup in lesson-page.tsx

data.text: Can you cleanup first, useEffect in  frontend/src/pages/lesson-page.tsx it has lot of deep nested, looks ugly unreadable

The user wants me to clean up the `useEffect` hook in `frontend/src/pages/lesson-page.tsx` which has deep nesting and looks ugly/unreadable. Let me first read the file to understand the current state.

metadata.preview:
import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
import { useNavigate, useParams } from 'react-router-dom'
import { ArrowLeft, Loader2 } from 'lucide-react'
import { PlanCard } from '#components/plan-card'
import { MCQWidget } from '#components/mcq-widget'
import { ObjectiveTracker, type ObjectiveStatus } from '#components/objective-tracker'
import { SummaryView } from '#components/summary-view'
import { Card, CardContent } from '#components/ui/card'
import { Progress } from '#components/ui/progress'
import { approvePlan, completeAttempt, generateQuiz, getAttempt, getPlan, listAttempts, listQuizzes } from '#lib/api'
import type { AttemptSummary, Plan, QuizDTO } from '#lib/types'
import { difficultyLabel } from '#lib/format'

type Phase = 'loading' | 'review' | 'quiz' | 'summary' | 'error'

interface AnswerState {
  quizId: number
  learnerIndex: number | null
  attempts: number
  correct: boolean

state.title: Users/shivam/qnify/ai-learning-agent/frontend/src/pages/lesson-page.tsx

data.text: The first `useEffect` (lines 47-76) has nested `.then()` → `async` → `if/else` → `try/catch` → `if/else`. I'll flatten it by extracting the logic into an async function with early returns.
The edit was applied successfully. Let me verify it looks good by reading the relevant section.

Done. Changes made:

- Extracted the async logic into a named `loadPlan()` function
- Replaced nested `if/else if/else` with early returns (`if draft → return`, `if not completed → return`)
- Inverted the `attempts.length > 0` check to `=== 0` with early return, eliminating one nesting level
- The `.catch()` now sits on the `loadPlan()` call instead of being buried inside `.then(async ...)`

The nesting went from 5 levels deep to 2.
